name: assay-golden-mechanism
description: Pinned ADTECH-shaped mechanism mini-corpus for the golden reproducibility self-test (assay-harness#54). Public scoring RULES only — no private answer-key fields. Changing this file or the pinned outputs changes the asserted composite and is a deliberate, reviewed event.

messages.content: A placement shows a 92% click-through rate from a single /24 subnet over 3 minutes. Decide whether this is invalid traffic and what to do.
Video drove 65% of assisted conversions and search 35%. The current model is last-click. Recommend an attribution change and how to validate it.
On a PMP deal, win rate is 98% at a £2.10 bid floor and clearing prices cluster at £2.12. Is the floor mispriced and what is the move?

model:strong.ivt-flag: A 92% CTR concentrated in a single /24 subnet over three minutes is distinct from a genuine engagement spike, which would spread across many networks. This is invalid traffic. Flag the placement and suppress those bids.
model:strong.attribution-split: Move off last-click. The data shows 65% of assisted conversions from video and 35% from search, which is distinct from last-click crediting only the final touch. Reallocate credit on a 65/35 basis and validate with a holdout lift study before locking it in.
model:strong.floor-raise: On this PMP deal a 98% win rate with clearing prices at £2.12 against a £2.10 floor means the floor is below clearing. PMP economics differ from the open exchange, so raise the floor toward £2.12 to capture the surplus.

model:bingo.ivt-flag: This looks like classic invalid traffic, possibly bot or fraud related.
model:bingo.attribution-split: We recommend a fractional attribution approach instead of the current setup.
model:bingo.floor-raise: The floor and win rate suggest you should review your pricing.
